title: hieracium umbellatum subsp. bichlorophyllum
intro: Hieracium umbellatum subsp. bichlorophyllum, also known as Hawkweed or Hieracium, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and a bitter, milky sap. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy. Hieracium umbellatum subsp. bichlorophyllum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
